## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. It is time to ditch the conspiracy theories Let's go So when I see this behavior from advertisers It's a sign that they lack confidence They lack confidence in their knowledge of how things work and their ability to find a solution So conspiracy theories relate to meta advertising take many forms examples I'm getting terrible results right now and so is everyone else or Meta's forcing audience expansion and advantage plus optimizations to take my money or this new feature or test That meta is running right now is causing results to tank red alert red alert The common thread is that there's something meta is doing or not doing that is hurting your results It removes your personal responsibility And it assumes this problem is affecting everyone and not just you so look There's a whole other topic about conspiracy theories. We're talking especially about meta advertising here But there are many reasons that these conspiracy theories catch on first of all, they're believable or at least Partially true right because look meta can be buggy Maybe the company hasn't been so trustworthy over the years Advantage plus optimizations sometimes can be counterproductive Depending on that optimization But the main reason they catch on in advertising circles is that they give us someone we can blame It provides some clarity. It's someone other than ourselves We don't want to be the reason that we're not getting good results That's an indictment on our ability to advertise if we can't pinpoint the wrong thing We're doing it must be meta So this stuff often catches on through social media with a simple question My results are tanking right now. So something's wrong. Anyone else guess what you are always going to get agreement There will always be an audience willing to agree that something is wrong that their results are tanking too That it must be an issue with meta and then the conspiracy theories start flying. So what did meta change recently? What is meta testing? What was recently buggy? These must be the reasons now There are a whole bunch of reasons why this approach is problematic But the main reason I ignore them is that they're born of chaos and they invite more chaos They're completely unproductive for a conspiracy theory to have truth It would mean that you have no control So I encourage you to ignore the conspiracy theories that won't give you any resolution

## [03:02 - 06:03]

Here's what I want you to do understand that you have nearly full control over your results Now that's sometimes scary It doesn't mean that there's an easy solution to find better results But it's an admission that when things aren't going well that you're part of the problem Ultimately, there's just so many things that we can change and where do we start? This is also why it's so important to understand how things work and to have a simplified approach So you're not chasing around a million different things that you can do if you need a little bit more background There about how to approach this and understanding really how these things work Make sure to listen to the last episode So if things aren't going well and honestly even when things are going well Understand that your results are most impacted by things you can do something about So your ad copy ad creative informat your offer Which is often probably most critical, but also the landing page Where are you sending people to is that a complete mess? Is it unprofessional? Is it broken? There's a load too slowly and even the product itself. Maybe people just don't want this thing Maybe it's a flop no matter how you spin it. So all those things can be good and bad, right? You can get amazing results because you've nailed those things or maybe you're getting bad results Because one or more of those things is an issue that you can improve upon You could take a completely hands-off and simplified approach Implementing most of the advanced post optimizations If not all and you could limit your campaigns and ad sets and yet you'll still have a lot of potential reasons for your performance If it's not going the way that you want It's not necessarily because of meta and honestly if the problem is that you can't trust meta You probably should not be advertising with meta So once you accept this responsibility and you push away the chaos of these conspiracy theories The difference is enormous Everything then is within your control You can do something about it and take pride in the results that you get You may know by now that I'm a bit of a content creation machine All right, so it's the blog posts. It's the short form videos It's the podcast episodes if you were to subscribe to each of those individual things which you can You could potentially get an email from me every single day and for some people they welcome that and they do that And I appreciate them a whole lot But you may be one of those people who doesn't like getting 67 emails from me every single week and you're like, okay enough John What you really need is a recap a weekly recap that goes out once per week with a summary of Everything that I published links to those things and then you're all caught up
